papers.title: An Ultrastrong and Ductile Duplex Lightweight Steel with Dual-Nanoprecipitation
papers.paper_scope: Investigation of microstructure and mechanical properties of Fe-30Mn-9Al-1C-1V-5Ni duplex lightweight steel with dual-nanoprecipitation (L'12 ordered domains and VC carbides) produced by cold rolling and heat treatment, focusing on strengthening and strain hardening mechanisms.

alloys.alloys_notes: Measured composition (wt.%, Table 1): Mn 30.45, Al 9.50, C 1.20, V 1.09, Ni 4.94, P 0.0040, S 0.0044, Fe Bal.

processes.description: Homogenized at 1050°C for 1 h, hot-rolled to 3 mm (85% reduction), water-quenched, cold-rolled to 1.5 mm (50% total reduction, single pass ≤5%), solution-treated at 900°C for 15 min, water-quenched.

processing_steps.processing_steps_notes: Hot rolled to 3 mm thickness, immediately water-quenched
Cold rolled to 1.5 mm final thickness

structures.overall_structure: Duplex microstructure of austenite (γ-FCC) and B2 (ordered BCC) phases, with nanoscale L'12 ordered domains (average size 1.0±0.3 nm, volume fraction 11.10%) and VC carbides (average size 19.9±6.2 nm, volume fraction 0.26%) within the austenite.
structures.microstructure_counts: Austenite ~80%, B2 20%, VC 0.26%, LRO domains 11.10% (within austenite)

phases_present.morphology: long strip-like large grains and fine particles decorating austenite grain boundaries

defect_interaction.quality_description: during deformation, GNDs accumulate at interfaces causing HDI strengthening

interfaces.phase_evolution: During tensile deformation, dislocations shear the LRO domains reducing their volume fraction; VC precipitates impede dislocation motion; dislocation density increases in both phases and accumulates at phase boundaries, enhancing HDI hardening.

tensile_properties.stress_strain_curves: Engineering stress-strain curve shown in Figure 4a; true stress-strain and work hardening rate curves shown in Figure 4b

characterization_methods.purpose: Phase identification and volume fraction analysis
characterization_methods.key_findings: Identified austenite, B2, and VC phases; B2 fraction ~20 vol%

characterization_methods.purpose: Phase map, grain morphology, grain size distribution, and GND density measurement
characterization_methods.key_findings: Austenite equiaxed grains, B2 as long strip-like grains and fine particles; average austenite grain size 1.31 µm; initial GND densities γ: 5.36×10^13 m^-2, B2: 1.04×10^14 m^-2

characterization_methods.purpose: Nanoscale precipitate characterization, interface structure, and deformation substructure
characterization_methods.key_findings: LRO domains (1.0±0.3 nm, 11.10 vol%) and VC (19.9±6.2 nm, 0.26 vol%) in austenite; semi-coherent B2/austenite interface, coherent VC/austenite; dislocations shear LRO and accumulate at VC particles and phase boundaries

characterization_methods.purpose: Capture microstructural evolution and GND density changes during deformation
characterization_methods.key_findings: GND density increases with strain in both phases; austenite accumulates dislocations faster and surpasses B2 at 6% strain; Schmid factor significantly influences grain deformation

characterization_methods.purpose: Evaluate HDI stress evolution and Bauschinger effect
characterization_methods.key_findings: HDI stress increases with strain (from ~620 MPa at 1% to ~845 MPa at 10.4%); yield drop Δσ_y and hysteresis loops reflect HDI strengthening; at yielding, HDI stress extrapolated to 597 MPa

computational_details.simulation_method: Thermodynamic calculations using CALPHAD and analytical strengthening models
computational_details.validation_against_experiment: Equilibrium phase diagram predictions consistent with XRD and EBSD phase fractions; calculated yield strength matches experimental 1316 MPa

analytical_model.equation_role: σ_YS = σ_γ f_γ + σ_B2 f_B2 + σ_HDI (Equation 1)

analytical_model.parameters: based on measured composition (wt.%): C 1.20, Mn 30.45, Al 9.50, Ni 4.94, V 1.09; σ_ss,γ = 228 MPa

analytical_model.equation_role: σ_VC = 0.538 G b f_VC^(1/2) / D_VC ln(D_VC / 2b) (Equation 6)
analytical_model.parameters: G=70 GPa, b=0.260 nm, f_VC=0.0026, D_VC=19.9 nm → σ_VC = 92 MPa
yield HDI stress = 597 MPa (extrapolated from Figure 5d)

analytical_model.model_note: derived from difference after accounting for other mechanisms

unmapped_findings: The fracture surface exhibits dimples, indicating a ductile fracture mode (Figure 4a inset).

The work-hardening curve shows three distinct stages: Stage I (0-1% true strain) sharp decrease with rate dropping below zero; Stage II (1-4.3%) rapid increase; Stage III (4.3-12%) gradual decrease but work-hardening rate remains high at 2.7-3.7 GPa.
LUR tests reveal yield drop Δσ_y and hysteresis loops that enlarge with strain; HDI stress increases progressively from about 620 MPa at 1% strain to about 845 MPa at 10.4% strain.
The measured chemical composition (Table 1) is: Mn 30.45, Al 9.50, C 1.20, V 1.09, Ni 4.94, P 0.0040, S 0.0044, Fe Bal. (wt.%).
GND density evolution from in-situ EBSD: in austenite from 5.36×10^13 to 2.64×10^14 m^-2, in B2 from 1.04×10^14 to 2.51×10^14 m^-2 as strain increases from 0% to 6%.
The volume fraction of LRO domains decreases from 11.10% in the initial state after deformation due to dislocations shearing the ordered domains.
The steel was produced via a simple processing route (homogenization, hot rolling, cold rolling, solution treatment) without complex thermomechanical treatments.
Schmid factor distributions were employed to explain variations in GND densities among individual austenite and B2 grains.
The B2 phase has a bimodal grain size distribution with mean sizes of 1.02 µm and 3.38 µm.
